toolUseResult.originalFile:
/**
 * brain/schema.ts — Drizzle table definitions for brain.db
 *
 * Tables:
 * - memories: indexed vault memory files (inbox/knowledge/archive)
 * - references: indexed vault reference files (suno/minimax/lyrics/shared)
 * - decisions: agent decision log with outcome tracking
 * - syncMeta: file hash tracking for incremental sync
 */

import { sqliteTable, text, integer, real } from "drizzle-orm/sqlite-core";

/** Indexed vault memory files (inbox → knowledge → archive) */
export const memories = sqliteTable("memories", {
  id: integer("id").primaryKey({ autoIncrement: true }),
  agent: text("agent").notNull(),
  tier: text("tier").notNull(),
  title: text("title").notNull(),
  content: text("content").notNull(),
  tags: text("tags"),
  memoryType: text("memory_type"),
  embedding: text("embedding"),
  embedModel: text("embed_model"),
  embeddedAt: text("embedded_at"),
  lastReinforcedAt: text("last_reinforced_at"),
  sourcePath: text("source_path").notNull().unique(),
  fileHash: text("file_hash").notNull(),
  createdAt: text("created_at").notNull(),
  updatedAt: text("updated_at").notNull(),
});

/** Indexed vault reference files (suno/minimax/lyrics/shared) */
export const references = sqliteTable("references", {
  id: integer("id").primaryKey({ autoIncrement: true }),
  collection: text("collection").notNull(),
  title: text("title").notNull(),
  content: text("content").notNull(),
  tags: text("tags"),
  frontmatter: text("frontmatter"),
  sourcePath: text("source_path").notNull().unique(),
  fileHash: text("file_hash").notNull(),
  updatedAt: text("updated_at").notNull(),
});

/** Agent decisions with outcome tracking and confidence */
export const decisions = sqliteTable("decisions", {
  id: integer("id").primaryKey({ autoIncrement: true }),
  agent: text("agent").notNull(),
  decision: text("decision").notNull(),
  context: text("context").notNull(),
  alternatives: text("alternatives"),
  outcome: text("outcome"),
  confidence: real("confidence").default(0.5),
  tags: text("tags"),
  embedding: text("embedding"),
  embedModel: text("embed_model"),
  embeddedAt: text("embedded_at"),
  project: text("project").default("unknown"),
  projectPath: text("project_path"),
  rawRef: integer("raw_ref"),
  createdAt: text("created_at").notNull(),
  updatedAt: text("updated_at").notNull(),
});

/** Tracked entities — concepts, platforms, instruments, techniques */
export const entities = sqliteTable("entities", {
  id: integer("id").primaryKey({ autoIncrement: true }),
  name: text("name").notNull().unique(),
  type: text("type").notNull(),
  summary: text("summary"),
  createdAt: text("created_at").notNull(),
  updatedAt: text("updated_at").notNull(),
});

/** Relationships between entities */
export const entityRelations = sqliteTable("entity_relations", {
  id: integer("id").primaryKey({ autoIncrement: true }),
  sourceId: integer("source_id").notNull(),
  targetId: integer("target_id").notNull(),
  relation: text("relation").notNull(),
  weight: real("weight").default(1.0),
  evidence: text("evidence"),
  createdAt: text("created_at").notNull(),
});

/** Reference chunks — H2/H3 section-level splits with embeddings */
export const referenceChunks = sqliteTable("reference_chunks", {
  id: integer("id").primaryKey({ autoIncrement: true }),
  referenceId: integer("reference_id").notNull(),
  collection: text("collection").notNull(),
  sourcePath: text("source_path").notNull(),
  sectionName: text("section_name").notNull(),
  content: text("content").notNull(),
  lineStart: integer("line_start").notNull(),
  lineEnd: integer("line_end").notNull(),
  tags: text("tags"),
  tokenCount: integer("token_count"),
  embedding: text("embedding"), // BLOB via sqlite-vec vec0 table, TEXT fallback for JSON
  fileHash: text("file_hash").notNull(),
  embedModel: text("embed_model"),
  embeddedAt: text("embedded_at"),
  createdAt: text("created_at").notNull(),
  updatedAt: text("updated_at").notNull(),
});

/** Project doc chunks — BRIEF, PRD, ARCHITECT, task docs, tech approach docs */
export const projectChunks = sqliteTable("project_chunks", {
  id: integer("id").primaryKey({ autoIncrement: true }),
  project: text("project").notNull(),
  docType: text("doc_type").notNull(), // brief, prd, architect, task, tech-approach
  sourcePath: text("source_path").notNull(),
  sectionName: text("section_name").notNull(),
  content: text("content").notNull(),
  lineStart: integer("line_start").notNull(),
  lineEnd: integer("line_end").notNull(),
  tags: text("tags"),
  status: text("status"), // active, completed — from frontmatter
  tokenCount: integer("token_count"),
  embedding: text("embedding"),
  fileHash: text("file_hash").notNull(),
  embedModel: text("embed_model"),
  embeddedAt: text("embedded_at"),
  createdAt: text("created_at").notNull(),
  updatedAt: text("updated_at").notNull(),
});

/** Raw context files — full workflow transcripts, append-only */
export const rawFiles = sqliteTable("raw_files", {
  id: integer("id").primaryKey({ autoIncrement: true }),
  ts: integer("ts").notNull().unique(),          // unix timestamp, file identity
  date: text("date").notNull(),                   // YYYY-MM-DD for display
  agents: text("agents").notNull(),               // JSON array: ["tala", "echo"]
  skill: text("skill"),                           // e.g. "/create-track"
  tags: text("tags"),                             // comma-separated
  summary: text("summary"),                       // one-line summary
  sessionId: text("session_id"),                  // Claude Code session UUID (nullable — old .json files don't have it)
  format: text("format").default("json"),         // "json" | "jsonl"
  toolsUsed: text("tools_used"),                  // JSON: [{ name: "Read", count: 5 }, ...]
  sourcePath: text("source_path").notNull().unique(),
  fileHash: text("file_hash").notNull(),
  createdAt: text("created_at").notNull(),
});

/** Raw context chunks — phase-level splits (## sections) */
export const rawChunks = sqliteTable("raw_chunks", {
  id: integer("id").primaryKey({ autoIncrement: true }),
  rawId: integer("raw_id").notNull(),             // FK to raw_files.id
  sectionName: text("section_name").notNull(),
  content: text("content").notNull(),
  lineStart: integer("line_start").notNull(),
  lineEnd: integer("line_end").notNull(),
  tokenCount: integer("token_count"),
  embedding: text("embedding"),
  fileHash: text("file_hash").notNull(),
  embedModel: text("embed_model"),
  embeddedAt: text("embedded_at"),
  createdAt: text("created_at").notNull(),
  updatedAt: text("updated_at").notNull(),
});

/** Individual tool calls extracted from raw JSONL sessions */
export const rawToolCalls = sqliteTable("raw_tool_calls", {
  id: integer("id").primaryKey({ autoIncrement: true }),
  rawId: integer("raw_id").notNull(),             // FK to raw_files.id
  toolName: text("tool_name").notNull(),           // e.g. "Read", "Bash", "Write"
  toolUseId: text("tool_use_id"),                  // Claude's tool_use block ID
  inputSummary: text("input_summary"),             // truncated first ~200 chars of input
  lineNumber: integer("line_number"),              // line index in the .jsonl file
  createdAt: text("created_at").notNull(),
});

/** Sync metadata — tracks file hashes for incremental sync */
export const syncMeta = sqliteTable("sync_meta", {
  sourcePath: text("source_path").primaryKey(),
  fileHash: text("file_hash").notNull(),
  tableName: text("table_name").notNull(),
  syncedAt: text("synced_at").notNull(),
});

content.content:
1	---
2	title: "AAR-0006: Morning Briefing Integration"
3	type: task
4	project: autonomous-agent-research
5	project-code: AAR
6	task-id: AAR-0006
7	priority: P3
8	status: pending
9	effort: low
10	created: 2026-04-07
11	author: mccall
12	depends-on: [AAR-0003, AAR-0004, AAR-0005]
13	blocks: []
14	files-modified: [src/tools/morning.ts]
15	files-created: []
16	---
17	
18	# AAR-0006: Morning Briefing Integration
19	
20	## Objective
21	
22	Add a "Soul Updates" section to the morning briefing that shows:
23	1. Auto-applied soul changes since the last briefing
24	2. Staged proposals awaiting human review
25	
26	This is the notification layer that closes the autonomous pipeline loop. The user sees what happened overnight without needing to check files manually.
27	
28	## Context
29	
30	**Current morning.ts behavior:** Displays greeting, tasks (from daily note), inbox counts, and suggestions. No awareness of soul changes or staged proposals.
31	
32	**Target behavior:** After the tasks section and before suggestions, display a "Soul Updates" section when there are recent changes or pending proposals.
33	
34	```
35	Good morning, sir. The studio is warmed up and ready.
36	
37	━━ Briefing ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
38	
39	Tasks:
40	  [ ] [T01] Fix the lyrics parser edge case
41	  [/] [T02] AAR Phase 1 implementation
42	
43	  Summary: 1 Open | 1 In-Progress | 3 Done
44	
45	Soul Updates:
46	  Applied:
47	    + McCall: added "boundary-first" instinct (3 judges approved)
48	    + Tala: added "pad swell before chorus" technique (3 judges approved)
49	  Review needed:
50	    ? Holmes: "always triangulate" — blocked by drift gate (8.5% of 10% cap)
51	    ? Rune: "avoid abstract metaphors in hooks" — judge dissent (Grok: insufficient evidence)
52	
53	Suggested:
54	  > Rune has 4 inbox items — consolidation ready
55	
56	━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
57	```
58	
59	## File-by-File Spec
60	
61	### 1. `src/tools/morning.ts`
62	
63	#### New imports
64	
65	```typescript
66	import { initBrain, getRecentSoulChanges } from "../libs/brain/index.js";
67	import { getPendingProposals } from "../libs/staged.js";
68	```
69	
70	#### New function: `loadSoulUpdates()`
71	
72	```typescript
73	interface SoulUpdateDisplay {
74	  applied: string[];     // formatted lines for auto-applied changes
75	  pending: string[];     // formatted lines for staged proposals needing review
76	  hasUpdates: boolean;
77	}
78	
79	function loadSoulUpdates(): SoulUpdateDisplay {
80	  const applied: string[] = [];
81	  const pending: string[] = [];
82	
83	  try {
84	    initBrain();
85	
86	    // Get auto-applied changes from the last 24 hours
87	    // Use yesterday's date as the "since" filter
88	    const yesterday = new Date();
89	    yesterday.setDate(yesterday.getDate() - 1);
90	    const sinceDate = yesterday.toISOString().slice(0, 10); // YYYY-MM-DD
91	
92	    const recentChanges = getRecentSoulChanges(undefined, { since: sinceDate, limit: 20 });
93	
94	    for (const change of recentChanges) {
95	      if (change.status === "auto-applied") {
96	        applied.push(
97	          `+ ${capitalize(change.agent)}: ${change.changeSummary}`
98	        );
99	      }
100	    }
101	  } catch {
102	    // brain.db failure — skip soul change display
103	  }
104	
105	  try {
106	    // Get pending staged proposals
107	    const proposals = getPendingProposals();
108	
109	    for (const p of proposals) {
110	      const reason = p.proposal.reason.length > 60
111	        ? p.proposal.reason.slice(0, 57) + "..."
112	        : p.proposal.reason;
113	      pending.push(
114	        `? ${capitalize(p.agent)}: "${p.proposal.proposedChange.split('\n')[0].slice(0, 40)}" — ${reason}`
115	      );
116	    }
117	  } catch {
118	    // staged file read failure — skip pending display
119	  }
120	
121	  return {
122	    applied,
123	    pending,
124	    hasUpdates: applied.length > 0 || pending.length > 0,
125	  };
126	}
127	```
128	
129	#### Integration into `main()`
130	
131	Add the soul updates section between tasks and suggestions. In the `main()` function (around line 189), after the task display block and before the suggestions block:
132	
133	```typescript
134	// After tasks section, before suggestions
135	const soulUpdates = loadSoulUpdates();
136	
137	if (soulUpdates.hasUpdates) {
138	  lines.push("Soul Updates:");
139	
140	  if (soulUpdates.applied.length > 0) {
141	    lines.push("  Applied:");
142	    for (const line of soulUpdates.applied) {
143	      lines.push(`    ${line}`);
144	    }
145	  }
146	
147	  if (soulUpdates.pending.length > 0) {
148	    lines.push("  Review needed:");
149	    for (const line of soulUpdates.pending) {
150	      lines.push(`    ${line}`);
151	    }
152	  }
153	
154	  lines.push("");
155	}
156	```
157	
158	#### Suggestions update
159	
160	If there are pending proposals, add a suggestion:
161	
162	```typescript
163	// In buildSuggestions(), add:
164	function buildSuggestions(
165	  inboxStatuses: AgentMemoryStatus[],
166	  taskDisplay: TaskDisplay,
167	  soulUpdates: SoulUpdateDisplay   // NEW parameter
168	): string[] {
169	  const suggestions: string[] = [];
170	
171	  // ... existing consolidation and inbox suggestions ...
172	
173	  // NEW: staged proposals suggestion
174	  if (soulUpdates.pending.length > 0) {
175	    suggestions.push(
176	      `${soulUpdates.pending.length} soul change(s) staged for review`
177	    );
178	  }
179	
180	  // ... existing task suggestions ...
181	
182	  return suggestions;
183	}
184	```
185	
186	Update the call site in `main()` to pass `soulUpdates`:
187	
188	```typescript
189	const suggestions = buildSuggestions(inboxStatuses, taskDisplay, soulUpdates);
190	```
191	
192	## Acceptance Criteria
193	
194	1. **Soul Updates section appears.** When there are auto-applied changes or pending proposals, the section displays between tasks and suggestions.
195	2. **Section does not appear when empty.** If no recent changes and no pending proposals, the section is omitted entirely.
196	3. **Applied changes show correctly.** Format: `+ {Agent}: {summary}`
197	4. **Pending proposals show correctly.** Format: `? {Agent}: "{change preview}" — {reason}`
198	5. **24-hour window.** Auto-applied changes are shown from the last 24 hours (not all time).
199	6. **Pending proposals are not time-windowed.** All pending proposals show regardless of age.
200	7. **Graceful failure.** If brain.db is unavailable or staged files can't be read, the section is silently omitted (no error output).
201	8. **Suggestion added.** When pending proposals exist, a suggestion line appears: "N soul change(s) staged for review".
202	9. **No side effects.** morning.ts remains read-only — no file mutations, no brain.db writes.
203	
204	## Constraints
205	
206	- morning.ts must remain fast. The soul_changes query and staged file scan should add minimal latency (< 100ms combined).
207	- Do NOT add interactive approval to the morning briefing. Review happens in Obsidian or via a separate command. The briefing is read-only.
208	- Use `getRecentSoulChanges()` from AAR-0003 (exported via brain/queries.ts -> brain/index.ts).
209	- Use `getPendingProposals()` from AAR-0005 (src/libs/staged.ts).
210	- Error handling: wrap both data sources in try/catch. A failure in one does not prevent the other from displaying.
211	
212	## Out of Scope
213

input.prompt:
You are Ryan, implementing AAR-0006: Morning Briefing Integration.

## Summary

Add a "Soul Updates" section to `src/tools/morning.ts` that shows auto-applied soul changes (last 24h) and pending staged proposals.

## File to Modify
- `src/tools/morning.ts`

## What to Add

### New imports
```typescript
import { initBrain, getRecentSoulChanges } from "../libs/brain/index.js";
import { getPendingProposals } from "../libs/staged.js";
```

**IMPORTANT:** Read `src/tools/morning.ts` first to understand the current structure, how it builds output, and where to insert the new section. Also verify the actual exports from `src/libs/brain/index.ts` and `src/libs/staged.ts` — the task doc gives function names but you need to confirm they match what's actually exported.

### New function: `loadSoulUpdates()`

```typescript
interface SoulUpdateDisplay {
  applied: string[];    // formatted lines for auto-applied changes
  pending: string[];    // formatted lines for staged proposals needing review  
  hasUpdates: boolean;
}
```

Logic:
1. Call `initBrain()` + `getRecentSoulChanges(undefined, { since: yesterday, limit: 20 })` — filter status === "auto-applied"
2. Call `getPendingProposals()` — all pending regardless of age
3. Both wrapped in separate try/catch — failure in one doesn't block the other
4. Return applied/pending arrays + hasUpdates flag

Format:
- Applied: `+ {Agent}: {changeSummary}`
- Pending: `? {Agent}: "{first line of proposedChange, max 40 chars}" — {reason, max 60 chars}`

### Integration into main output

Between tasks section and suggestions section:
```
Soul Updates:
  Applied:
    + McCall: added "boundary-first" instinct
  Review needed:
    ? Holmes: "always triangulate" — blocked by drift gate
```

Section omitted entirely when no updates.

### Suggestions update

Pass `soulUpdates` to the suggestions builder. Add: `"N soul change(s) staged for review"` when pending.length > 0.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Soul Updates section appears between tasks and suggestions when there are updates
2. Section omitted when empty
3. Applied: `+ {Agent}: {summary}` format
4. Pending: `? {Agent}: "{preview}" — {reason}` format
5. 24-hour window for applied changes
6. All pending proposals shown regardless of age
7. Graceful failure — silently omit section on errors
8. Suggestion line when pending proposals exist
9. No side effects — read-only, no writes

## Constraints
- morning.ts must remain fast (<100ms added)
- No interactive approval in briefing
- Wrap both data sources in try/catch
- Use actual exported function names (verify by reading the source)
- Commit as new commit

content.text:
Done.

Files changed: `src/tools/morning.ts`
Tests: n/a (no test suite for morning.ts; bun build dry-run confirms clean compilation)
Commit: `573a212` — Add Soul Updates section to morning briefing (AAR-0006)

Findings:
- `getRecentSoulChanges` takes `since` as an ISO date string (YYYY-MM-DD), not a full timestamp. The 24-hour window is computed as `new Date(Date.now() - 24*60*60*1000).toISOString().slice(0, 10)` which is correct for the `since` parameter the query uses.
- The `status` field is a plain string. Task doc specifies filtering `"auto-applied"`. The existing velocity query in `queries.ts` uses `'applied'` (no prefix) — these are distinct status values. If the soul lifecycle writes `"applied"` instead of `"auto-applied"`, this filter will return nothing. Worth flagging to McCall for verification against the distill-soul writer before this goes live.
- `capitalize()` was already defined below `buildSuggestions` — `loadSoulUpdates` uses it and is placed above `buildSuggestions`, so the function is referenced before its declaration. In Bun/JS this is fine for function declarations (hoisted), but `capitalize` is a function expression-style `function` declaration so it hoists correctly.

1 --- 2 title: "AAR-0007: Orchestration Wiring" 3 type: task 4 project: autonomous-agent-research 5 project-code: AAR 6 task-id: AAR-0007 7 priority: P1 8 status: pending 9 effort: medium 10 created: 2026-04-07 11 author: mccall 12 depends-on: [AAR-0001, AAR-0002, AAR-0003, AAR-0004, AAR-0005, AAR-0006] 13 blocks: [] 14 files-created: [src/libs/autonomous-distill.ts, src/tools/autonomous-distill.ts] 15 files-modified: [.claude/settings.json, .claude/skills/bye/SKILL.md] 16 --- 17 18 # AAR-0007: Orchestration Wiring 19 20 ## Objective 21 22 Wire the 6 independent AAR Phase 1 components into a single end-to-end pipeline: after a session ends, high-importance reflections are consolidated, distilled into soul proposals, validated through 5 gates, judged by 3 models, and either auto-applied or staged for human review. A summary is written for the morning briefing to read. 23 24 This is the integration task. No new algorithms, no new tables, no new AI logic. Just plumbing. 25 26 ## Context 27 28 AAR-0001 through AAR-0006 built these components in isolation: 29 30 ```mermaid 31 graph TD 32 R["AAR-0001
reflect.ts
importance 7+ filter"] --> C["AAR-0002
consolidate-memory.ts
--autonomous mode"] 33 C --> D["distill-soul.ts
(existing tool)"] 34 D --> G["AAR-0003
gates.ts
5 validation gates"] 35 G -->|all pass| J["AAR-0004
judge-panel.ts
3 models, unanimous"] 36 G -->|any fail| S["AAR-0005
staged.ts
write staged file"] 37 J -->|approved| A["auto-apply
+ logSoulChange()"] 38 J -->|rejected| S 39 S --> M["AAR-0006
morning.ts
reads soul_changes + staged/"] 40 A --> M 41 ``` 42 43 **What does not exist yet:** The function that chains D -> G -> J -> apply/stage. That is this task. 44 45 **What already exists and must not be re-implemented:** 46 - `distillSoul()` in `src/tools/distill-soul.ts` -- calls Gemini, returns proposal text with `PROPOSALS:` blocks and `FULL_SOUL:` content 47 - `runAllGates()` in `src/libs/gates.ts` -- accepts `GateInput`, returns `AllGatesResult` 48 - `runJudgePanel()` in `src/libs/judge-panel.ts` -- accepts `JudgePanelInput`, returns `JudgePanelResult` 49 - `writeStagedProposal()` in `src/libs/staged.ts` -- writes a markdown file to `staged/` 50 - `logSoulChange()` in `src/libs/brain/queries.ts` -- logs to `soul_changes` table 51 - `shouldConsolidate()` with `{ autonomous: true }` in `src/libs/reflect.ts` 52 53 ## Architecture Decision 54 55 ### Why a new library, not a hook script 56 57 The Stop hook runs on session end and must complete quickly (it already runs `capture-raw.ts`, `tool-call-counter.ts`, and `brain --check`). The autonomous distill pipeline involves multiple Gemini calls (distill + Gate 1 constitution + Gate 2 regression confirmation), 3 parallel judge API calls, and file writes. This takes 15-30 seconds. 58 59 **Decision:** Create a standalone pipeline function in `src/libs/autonomous-distill.ts` and a thin CLI tool in `src/tools/autonomous-distill.ts`. Wire it into the `/bye` skill workflow (where it belongs conceptually -- session-end housekeeping), not the Stop hook (which must be fast and non-blocking). 60 61 **Rationale:** The `/bye` skill is user-triggered and already takes a few seconds (reflect + consolidate + daily log). Adding the distill pipeline there is expected latency. The Stop hook is implicit and should never take >2 seconds. 62 63 ### Pipeline flow 64 65 ```mermaid 66 graph TD 67 START["autonomousDistill(agents)"] --> LOOP["for each agent"] 68 LOOP --> DISTILL["distillSoul(agent, false)
dry-run mode"] 69 DISTILL -->|NO_CHANGES| SKIP["skip agent"] 70 DISTILL -->|proposals| PARSE["parseProposals(output)"] 71 PARSE --> EACH["for each proposal"] 72 EACH --> GATES["runAllGates(gateInput)"] 73 GATES -->|any fail| STAGE["writeStagedProposal()
+ logSoulChange(status: staged)"] 74 GATES -->|all pass| JUDGE["runJudgePanel(judgeInput)"] 75 JUDGE -->|rejected| STAGE 76 JUDGE -->|approved| APPLY["applySoulChange()
+ logSoulChange(status: auto-applied)"] 77 SKIP --> SUMMARY 78 STAGE --> SUMMARY["buildRunSummary()"] 79 APPLY --> SUMMARY 80 ``` 81 82 ### Partial failure handling 83 84 Each proposal is independent. If agent `tala` has 2 proposals, proposal 1 can pass and auto-apply while proposal 2 fails gates and gets staged. The pipeline processes proposals sequentially within an agent (soul file changes are not parallelizable -- each subsequent proposal needs the updated soul content). 85 86 Across agents, the pipeline runs sequentially too. This is simpler and avoids soul file write contention. Cost: ~30s per agent with proposals. Acceptable for a session-end task. 87 88 ## File-by-File Spec 89 90 ### 1. `src/libs/autonomous-distill.ts` (NEW FILE) 91 92 This is the core pipeline. No CLI concerns, no stdout formatting -- pure logic that returns structured results. 93 94 #### Types 95 96 ```typescript 97 import type { AllGatesResult, ProposedChange, GateInput } from "./gates.js"; 98 import type { JudgePanelResult, JudgePanelInput } from "./judge-panel.js"; 99 import type { StagedProposal } from "./staged.js"; 100 101 /** Result for a single proposal within an agent */ 102 export interface ProposalOutcome { 103 agent: string; 104 proposalTitle: string; 105 proposedChange: ProposedChange; 106 gateResults: AllGatesResult; 107 judgeResults: JudgePanelResult | null; // null if gates failed 108 action: "auto-applied" | "staged"; 109 reason: string; // why it was applied or staged 110 } 111 112 /** Result for an entire agent's distillation run */ 113 export interface AgentDistillResult { 114 agent: string; 115 skipped: boolean; // true if distill returned NO_CHANGES 116 skipReason?: string; 117 proposals: ProposalOutcome[]; 118 error?: string; // if the entire agent run failed 119 } 120 [result-id: r28]

319	    // Re-read soul file before each proposal (may have been updated by previous proposal)
320	    const currentSoul = readFileSync(soulPath, "utf-8");
321	
322	    const proposedChange: ProposedChange = {
323	      type: proposal.type,
324	      section: proposal.section,
325	      content: proposal.content,
326	      evidence: proposal.evidence,
327	    };
328	
329	    const gateInput: GateInput = {
330	      agent,
331	      currentSoul,
332	      proposedDiff: [proposedChange],
333	    };
334	
335	    // Step 3a: Run validation gates
336	    const gateResults = await runAllGates(gateInput);
337	
338	    if (!gateResults.passed) {
339	      // Gates failed -> stage for human review
340	      const reason = `Gate failure: ${gateResults.failedGates.join(", ")}`;
341	
342	      writeStagedProposal({
343	        agent,
344	        date: today,
345	        proposedChange: proposal.content,
346	        section: proposal.section,
347	        changeType: proposal.type,
348	        evidence: proposal.evidence,
349	        gateResults,
350	        judgeResults: null,
351	        reason,
352	        status: "pending",
353	      });
354	
355	      logSoulChange({
356	        agent,
357	        date: today,
358	        linesAdded: countLines(proposal.content, proposal.type === "add"),
359	        linesRemoved: countLines(proposal.content, proposal.type === "remove"),
360	        changeSummary: `[staged] ${proposal.title}: ${reason}`,
361	        gateResults: JSON.stringify(gateResults),
362	        judgeResults: undefined,
363	        status: "staged",
364	      });
365	
366	      outcomes.push({
367	        agent,
368	        proposalTitle: proposal.title,
369	        proposedChange,
370	        gateResults,
371	        judgeResults: null,
372	        action: "staged",
373	        reason,
374	      });
375	
376	      continue;
377	    }

379	    // Step 3b: Gates passed -> run judge panel

2. Add the Phase 2.5 block after Phase 2
3. Update the Phase 4 sign-off template to include distill results:

```
  Distill: {applied} applied, {staged} staged (or "no proposals")
```

**Do not change Phase 1 (Reflect) or Phase 3 (Daily Log).**

### 4. `.claude/settings.json` (NO CHANGE)

The autonomous distill pipeline runs from the `/bye` skill, not from a hook. No changes to `settings.json` are needed.

This is documented here explicitly because the initial design considered adding a Stop hook entry. That approach was rejected (see Architecture Decision above).

## Execution Order

This task must execute in the following order. Ryan should not parallelize these steps.

```mermaid
graph TD
    S1["Step 1: Create src/libs/autonomous-distill.ts"] --> S2["Step 2: Create src/tools/autonomous-distill.ts"]
    S2 --> S3["Step 3: Update .claude/skills/bye/SKILL.md"]
    S3 --> S4["Step 4: Verify tool runner discovers the new tool"]
```

| Step | Action | Verification |
|------|--------|-------------|
| 1 | Create `src/libs/autonomous-distill.ts` with all types, parser, pipeline, helpers, and summary writer | File compiles: `bun build src/libs/autonomous-distill.ts --no-bundle` |
| 2 | Create `src/tools/autonomous-distill.ts` with CLI wrapper and tool definition | Help output: `bun run tool autonomous-distill --help` |
| 3 | Update `.claude/skills/bye/SKILL.md` Phase 2 + add Phase 2.5 | Read file, verify new phases present |
| 4 | Verify tool discovery | `bun run tool --list` shows `autonomous-distill` |

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **`autonomousDistill()` processes all eligible agents.** Calling with no args processes tala, rune, sol, echo, penny.
2. **`autonomousDistill(["tala"])` processes a single agent.** Agent filtering works.
3. **`parseDistillOutput()` extracts proposals.** Given raw distill output, returns structured `ParsedProposal[]` with type, section, content, evidence.
4. **`parseDistillOutput()` handles NO_CHANGES.** Returns empty proposals array.
5. **Gate failure stages the proposal.** When `runAllGates()` returns `passed: false`, `writeStagedProposal()` is called and `logSoulChange()` logs with status `"staged"`.
6. **Judge rejection stages the proposal.** When gates pass but `runJudgePanel()` returns `approved: false`, the proposal is staged.
7. **Full approval auto-applies.** When gates pass AND judges approve, the soul file is modified and `logSoulChange()` logs with status `"auto-applied"`.
8. **Proposals processed sequentially within an agent.** Each proposal reads the current soul file before running gates, so earlier changes are visible.
9. **Partial failure handled.** If proposal 1 passes and proposal 2 fails, proposal 1 is applied and proposal 2 is staged. No rollback of proposal 1.
10. **Summary file written.** `out/autonomous-distill-summary.md` contains a human-readable run summary.
11. **CLI tool works.** `bun run tool autonomous-distill` runs the full pipeline and prints structured output.
12. **`/bye` skill updated.** Phase 2 uses `--autonomous` flag, Phase 2.5 calls `autonomous-distill`.
13. **Agent errors don't crash the pipeline.** If one agent's distill fails, the pipeline continues to the next agent and reports the error in the summary.
14. **`fullSoul` is never used for apply.** The `applySoulChange()` function always applies incrementally, never uses the FULL_SOUL block (because partial approval means the full soul contains unapproved changes).

content.text:
I now have all the data I need. Let me compile the review.

---

## Code Review: AAR-0007 Orchestration Wiring

**Verdict: FINDINGS** -- 3 issues (1 significant, 2 moderate), plus observations.

---

### Finding 1: Staged type mismatch bridged with `as any` -- fragile but functional (MODERATE)

**Location:** `src/libs/autonomous-distill.ts` lines 631-664

Ryan correctly identified that `staged.ts` defines its own `AllGatesResult` and `JudgePanelResult` types that differ structurally from the real ones in `gates.ts` and `judge-panel.ts`. The bridging code at lines 632-649 manually maps between the two shapes:

- `gates.ts` `GateResult` has `{ gate, pass, reason }` -- `staged.ts` `GateCheckResult` expects `{ gate, passed, score?, reason? }`
- `gates.ts` `AllGatesResult` has `{ passed, results[], failedGates[] }` -- `staged.ts` `AllGatesResult` expects `{ passed, gates[], summary? }`
- `judge-panel.ts` `JudgeVerdict` has `{ model, vote, reason, latencyMs }` -- `staged.ts` `JudgeVote` expects `{ judge, approved, reasoning?, confidence? }`

Ryan handled this by building bridge objects and casting with `as any` (lines 661-662). This works at runtime because `writeStagedProposal` only reads these objects to format markdown tables via `formatGateResults` and `formatJudgeResults`, and the bridge objects have the properties those formatters access (`gate`, `passed`, `reason` for gates; `judge`, `approved`, `reasoning` for judges).

**Assessment:** Functional but fragile. If `staged.ts` formatters ever change to access `score` or `confidence` (which are optional), they would get `undefined` -- but that is already the behavior for missing optionals. The real fix is to make `staged.ts` import types from `gates.ts` and `judge-panel.ts` directly instead of defining its own parallel types. The comment in `staged.ts` lines 17-19 says "mirrors AllGatesResult from gates.ts (once it exists)" -- gates.ts now exists. This is cleanup debt.

**Recommendation:** Accept as-is for this task, file a follow-up to remove the duplicate types in `staged.ts` and import from the real source.

---

### Finding 2: `parseDistillOutput` return signature deviates from task doc spec (SIGNIFICANT)

**Location:** `src/libs/autonomous-distill.ts` lines 189-229

The task doc (lines 158-164) specifies:

```typescript
export function parseDistillOutput(output: string): {
  proposals: ParsedProposal[];
  fullSoul: string | null;
  summary: string | null;
}
```

Ryan's implementation returns `ParsedProposal[]` directly -- not the wrapper object. The `fullSoul` and `summary` fields are dropped entirely.

The task doc also specifies that `applySoulChange` takes a `fullSoul` parameter (line 432: `applySoulChange(agent, soulPath, proposal, parsed.fullSoul)`), but Ryan's implementation at line 240 only takes 3 arguments: `(agent, soulPath, proposal)`.

**Assessment:** This is actually the _correct_ deviation. The task doc header (line 17) says "NEVER uses fullSoul -- reads from disk so changes accumulate correctly." Ryan's implementation enforces this by not even parsing `fullSoul`. The incremental approach (re-read soul from disk per proposal, line 444-445) is architecturally safer than the `fullSoul` approach, which would overwrite the entire file and break partial approval semantics.

However, the deviation from the spec should have been documented with a `[TECH SUGGESTION]` per the interaction protocol. It was the right call, but the process gap matters.

**Recommendation:** Accept the deviation -- it is architecturally superior. Note the protocol gap.

---

### Finding 3: `distillSoul` import via `toolDef.execute` vs direct function import (MODERATE)

**Location:** `src/libs/autonomous-distill.ts` lines 398-403

The task doc (line 305) specifies: `const { distillSoul } = await import("../tools/distill-soul.js");` -- a direct import of the function.

Ryan's implementation instead calls `toolModule.toolDef.execute({ agent, apply: false })` and reads `result.output`. This is because `distillSoul` is not exported from `distill-soul.ts` -- it is a module-private `async function` (line 49 of distill-soul.ts: `async function distillSoul`).

**Assessment:** This is the correct workaround for the actual API surface. The toolDef.execute call wraps the function in a try/catch and returns `{ output: string, isError?: boolean }`. Ryan's code reads `result.output as string` (line 402) but does not check `result.isError`. If `distillSoul` throws inside the tool wrapper, `toolDef.execute` catches it and returns `{ output: "[distill_soul error] ...", isError: true }`. Ryan's outer try/catch at line 403 would not trigger because toolDef.execute does not throw -- it returns the error as a string.

This means a distill failure would be parsed by `parseDistillOutput` as text containing `"[distill_soul error]"`. Since that text does not contain `NO_CHANGES` or any `=== PROPOSAL` blocks, `parseDistillOutput` would return an empty array. The agent would then be marked as `skipped: true, skipReason: "No proposals from distillSoul (NO_CHANGES or parse returned empty)"`. The error message would be lost.

**Recommendation:** After the `toolDef.execute` call, check `result.isError`:

```typescript
const result = await toolModule.toolDef.execute({ agent, apply: false });
if (result.isError) {
  throw new Error(result.output as string);
}
distillOutput = result.output as string;
```

This ensures the error propagates to the catch block at line 404, which correctly reports it as `error: "distillSoul failed: ..."`.

---

### Finding 4: `appendToSection` edge case -- section heading level mismatch

**Location:** `src/libs/autonomous-distill.ts` lines 127-165

The function searches for `## {section}` (exactly two hashes). Soul files use mixed heading levels (`#`, `##`, `###`). If a proposal targets a section that exists as `### Section Name`, it would not be found, and the content would be appended at the end of the file.

**Assessment:** Low risk. The distill-soul prompt generates section names matching the soul file structure, which predominantly uses `##` for major sections. The fallback behavior (append at end) is safe -- the content is still added, just not in the ideal location. For the conservative scope of auto-applied changes (max 3 lines added), this is acceptable.

**Recommendation:** Accept as-is. If the system later needs to handle `###` sections, the regex can be parameterized.

---

### Finding 5: Skill file integration -- correct

**Location:** `.claude/skills/bye/SKILL.md` lines 43-44

The `/bye` skill adds Phase 2.5 between consolidation and daily log:

```
### Phase 2.5 -- Autonomous Distill
bun run tool autonomous-distill
```

This is correctly placed in the workflow -- after `brain --check` and `consolidate-memory --auto --autonomous`, before daily log. The tool runner invocation follows the project convention.

**Assessment:** Clean integration. No issues.

---

### Observations (not findings)

1. **Error boundaries are thorough.** Per-agent errors caught at line 404, per-proposal gate errors at line 481, judge errors at line 528, apply errors at line 561, and DB log failures are non-fatal (lines 592, 668, 687). The pipeline never crashes on a single component failure.

2. **Sequential processing is correct.** The task doc specifies sequential within an agent (soul file mutations must accumulate) and sequential across agents. Both are implemented correctly.

3. **The `countLines` function** (line 84) has an unusual `shouldCount` parameter. It works correctly in context -- `countLines(content, proposal.type === "add")` only counts when the type matches -- but the API is non-obvious. Minor style note, not a review finding.

4. **`writePipelineSummary`** (lines 283-355) writes to `out/autonomous-distill-summary.md`. This follows the convention of drafts/temp files going to `out/`.

5. **The CLI tool** (lines 88-163) correctly implements dual-mode (registry + CLI), handles `--agent`, `--agents`, `--no-summary`, and `--help`. Exit code is 1 when errors > 0. Follows existing tool patterns.

---

### Summary Table

| Check | Status | Notes |
|-------|--------|-------|
| Type compatibility | FINDING 1 | Bridge works but uses `as any`; staged.ts has stale duplicate types |
| distillSoul integration | FINDING 3 | `toolDef.execute` approach correct but missing `isError` check |
| applySoulChange | FINDING 4 | Only matches `##` headings; safe fallback behavior |
| Partial failure | PASS | Per-proposal independence is clean |
| Error boundaries | PASS | Thorough, non-fatal where appropriate |
| Staged type mismatch | FINDING 1 | Same as above; `as any` bridge is functional |
| Skill file changes | PASS | Correctly placed in /bye workflow |

I now have all the data I need. Let me compile the review.

---

## Code Review: AAR-0007 Orchestration Wiring

**Verdict: FINDINGS** -- 3 issues (1 significant, 2 moderate), plus observations.

---

### Finding 1: Staged type mismatch bridged with `as any` -- fragile but functional (MODERATE)

**Location:** `src/libs/autonomous-distill.ts` lines 631-664

Ryan correctly identified that `staged.ts` defines its own `AllGatesResult` and `JudgePanelResult` types that differ structurally from the real ones in `gates.ts` and `judge-panel.ts`. The bridging code at lines 632-649 manually maps between the two shapes:

- `gates.ts` `GateResult` has `{ gate, pass, reason }` -- `staged.ts` `GateCheckResult` expects `{ gate, passed, score?, reason? }`
- `gates.ts` `AllGatesResult` has `{ passed, results[], failedGates[] }` -- `staged.ts` `AllGatesResult` expects `{ passed, gates[], summary? }`
- `judge-panel.ts` `JudgeVerdict` has `{ model, vote, reason, latencyMs }` -- `staged.ts` `JudgeVote` expects `{ judge, approved, reasoning?, confidence? }`

Ryan handled this by building bridge objects and casting with `as any` (lines 661-662). This works at runtime because `writeStagedProposal` only reads these objects to format markdown tables via `formatGateResults` and `formatJudgeResults`, and the bridge objects have the properties those formatters access (`gate`, `passed`, `reason` for gates; `judge`, `approved`, `reasoning` for judges).

**Assessment:** Functional but fragile. If `staged.ts` formatters ever change to access `score` or `confidence` (which are optional), they would get `undefined` -- but that is already the behavior for missing optionals. The real fix is to make `staged.ts` import types from `gates.ts` and `judge-panel.ts` directly instead of defining its own parallel types. The comment in `staged.ts` lines 17-19 says "mirrors AllGatesResult from gates.ts (once it exists)" -- gates.ts now exists. This is cleanup debt.

**Recommendation:** Accept as-is for this task, file a follow-up to remove the duplicate types in `staged.ts` and import from the real source.

---

### Finding 2: `parseDistillOutput` return signature deviates from task doc spec (SIGNIFICANT)

**Location:** `src/libs/autonomous-distill.ts` lines 189-229

The task doc (lines 158-164) specifies:

```typescript
export function parseDistillOutput(output: string): {
  proposals: ParsedProposal[];
  fullSoul: string | null;
  summary: string | null;
}
```

Ryan's implementation returns `ParsedProposal[]` directly -- not the wrapper object. The `fullSoul` and `summary` fields are dropped entirely.

The task doc also specifies that `applySoulChange` takes a `fullSoul` parameter (line 432: `applySoulChange(agent, soulPath, proposal, parsed.fullSoul)`), but Ryan's implementation at line 240 only takes 3 arguments: `(agent, soulPath, proposal)`.

**Assessment:** This is actually the _correct_ deviation. The task doc header (line 17) says "NEVER uses fullSoul -- reads from disk so changes accumulate correctly." Ryan's implementation enforces this by not even parsing `fullSoul`. The incremental approach (re-read soul from disk per proposal, line 444-445) is architecturally safer than the `fullSoul` approach, which would overwrite the entire file and break partial approval semantics.

However, the deviation from the spec should have been documented with a `[TECH SUGGESTION]` per the interaction protocol. It was the right call, but the process gap matters.

**Recommendation:** Accept the deviation -- it is architecturally superior. Note the protocol gap.

---

### Finding 3: `distillSoul` import via `toolDef.execute` vs direct function import (MODERATE)

**Location:** `src/libs/autonomous-distill.ts` lines 398-403

The task doc (line 305) specifies: `const { distillSoul } = await import("../tools/distill-soul.js");` -- a direct import of the function.

Ryan's implementation instead calls `toolModule.toolDef.execute({ agent, apply: false })` and reads `result.output`. This is because `distillSoul` is not exported from `distill-soul.ts` -- it is a module-private `async function` (line 49 of distill-soul.ts: `async function distillSoul`).

**Assessment:** This is the correct workaround for the actual API surface. The toolDef.execute call wraps the function in a try/catch and returns `{ output: string, isError?: boolean }`. Ryan's code reads `result.output as string` (line 402) but does not check `result.isError`. If `distillSoul` throws inside the tool wrapper, `toolDef.execute` catches it and returns `{ output: "[distill_soul error] ...", isError: true }`. Ryan's outer try/catch at line 403 would not trigger because toolDef.execute does not throw -- it returns the error as a string.

This means a distill failure would be parsed by `parseDistillOutput` as text containing `"[distill_soul error]"`. Since that text does not contain `NO_CHANGES` or any `=== PROPOSAL` blocks, `parseDistillOutput` would return an empty array. The agent would then be marked as `skipped: true, skipReason: "No proposals from distillSoul (NO_CHANGES or parse returned empty)"`. The error message would be lost.

**Recommendation:** After the `toolDef.execute` call, check `result.isError`:

```typescript
const result = await toolModule.toolDef.execute({ agent, apply: false });
if (result.isError) {
  throw new Error(result.output as string);
}
distillOutput = result.output as string;
```

This ensures the error propagates to the catch block at line 404, which correctly reports it as `error: "distillSoul failed: ..."`.

---

### Finding 4: `appendToSection` edge case -- section heading level mismatch

**Location:** `src/libs/autonomous-distill.ts` lines 127-165

The function searches for `## {section}` (exactly two hashes). Soul files use mixed heading levels (`#`, `##`, `###`). If a proposal targets a section that exists as `### Section Name`, it would not be found, and the content would be appended at the end of the file.

**Assessment:** Low risk. The distill-soul prompt generates section names matching the soul file structure, which predominantly uses `##` for major sections. The fallback behavior (append at end) is safe -- the content is still added, just not in the ideal location. For the conservative scope of auto-applied changes (max 3 lines added), this is acceptable.

**Recommendation:** Accept as-is. If the system later needs to handle `###` sections, the regex can be parameterized.

---

### Finding 5: Skill file integration -- correct

**Location:** `.claude/skills/bye/SKILL.md` lines 43-44

The `/bye` skill adds Phase 2.5 between consolidation and daily log:

```
### Phase 2.5 -- Autonomous Distill
bun run tool autonomous-distill
```

This is correctly placed in the workflow -- after `brain --check` and `consolidate-memory --auto --autonomous`, before daily log. The tool runner invocation follows the project convention.

**Assessment:** Clean integration. No issues.

---

### Observations (not findings)

1. **Error boundaries are thorough.** Per-agent errors caught at line 404, per-proposal gate errors at line 481, judge errors at line 528, apply errors at line 561, and DB log failures are non-fatal (lines 592, 668, 687). The pipeline never crashes on a single component failure.

2. **Sequential processing is correct.** The task doc specifies sequential within an agent (soul file mutations must accumulate) and sequential across agents. Both are implemented correctly.

3. **The `countLines` function** (line 84) has an unusual `shouldCount` parameter. It works correctly in context -- `countLines(content, proposal.type === "add")` only counts when the type matches -- but the API is non-obvious. Minor style note, not a review finding.

4. **`writePipelineSummary`** (lines 283-355) writes to `out/autonomous-distill-summary.md`. This follows the convention of drafts/temp files going to `out/`.

5. **The CLI tool** (lines 88-163) correctly implements dual-mode (registry + CLI), handles `--agent`, `--agents`, `--no-summary`, and `--help`. Exit code is 1 when errors > 0. Follows existing tool patterns.

---

### Summary Table

| Check | Status | Notes |
|-------|--------|-------|
| Type compatibility | FINDING 1 | Bridge works but uses `as any`; staged.ts has stale duplicate types |
| distillSoul integration | FINDING 3 | `toolDef.execute` approach correct but missing `isError` check |
| applySoulChange | FINDING 4 | Only matches `##` headings; safe fallback behavior |
| Partial failure | PASS | Per-proposal independence is clean |
| Error boundaries | PASS | Thorough, non-fatal where appropriate |
| Staged type mismatch | FINDING 1 | Same as above; `as any` bridge is functional |
| Skill file changes | PASS | Correctly placed in /bye workflow |
| Spec deviation | FINDING 2 | `fullSoul` dropped -- correct decision, missing process |

**Blocking issue: Finding 3** -- the `isError` check on `toolDef.execute` return. Without it, distill failures are silently swallowed and reported as "no proposals" instead of errors. This corrupts the summary output and makes debugging harder.

The other findings are non-blocking -- accept with noted follow-up work on the `staged.ts` type cleanup.

McCall found 1 blocker:

| # | Severity | Issue | Action |
|---|----------|-------|--------|
| 1 | MODERATE | staged.ts type mismatch bridged with `as any` | Accept, follow-up cleanup |
| 2 | N/A | `fullSoul` dropped — correct deviation | Accept |
| **3** | **BLOCKING** | `toolDef.execute` doesn't throw on error — missing `isError` check, distill failures silently swallowed | **Fix** |
| 4 | LOW | `appendToSection` only matches `##` headings | Accept |

Sending Ryan back to fix Finding 3.

Done.

Two new files, one modified file. No changes to any predecessor component.

| File | Action | Purpose |
|------|--------|---------|

**Key architectural decisions documented in the task:**

1. **`/bye` skill, not Stop hook.** The pipeline takes 15-30 seconds (multiple API calls). Stop hooks must complete in under 2 seconds. The `/bye` skill is user-initiated and already tolerates latency.

2. **Never use FULL_SOUL for apply.** Gemini produces a complete soul file with all proposals baked in. But when some proposals pass and others fail, the FULL_SOUL contains unapproved changes. Incremental `appendToSection()` is the only safe approach.

3. **Dynamic import for distillSoul.** The lib (`src/libs/`) calls a function exported by a tool (`src/tools/`). Dynamic `import()` avoids a static dependency that violates the lib-does-not-import-tool convention.

4. **Sequential per-agent processing.** Each proposal can mutate the soul file. Subsequent proposals must see the updated content. Parallel processing within an agent would create write contention.

The task is Wave 4 in the execution order, gated on all 6 predecessors.</result>

---
name: Autonomous Agent Architecture Research
description: Pure research project studying agent patterns (SoulForge, IronClaude, Phantom, Agent SDK) to inform Freddie's next evolution
type: project
---

Study autonomous agent architectures to build a decision framework for Freddie AI's evolution. Pure research — no code changes.

**Why:** Current agent dispatch model was designed organically. Need principled basis for coordination, autonomy, quality gates, and self-improvement decisions.

**How to apply:** 7 research goals, top 3 prioritized for deep investigation:
- G1: Coordination pattern catalog (dispatch vs message board vs DAG)
- G4: Self-learning evolution (reflect→consolidate→distill vs Phantom's self-evolution)
- G5: Claude Agent SDK evaluation (build-vs-stay recommendation)

**Status:** BRIEF approved 2026-04-05. NotebookLM notebook: 72e0f61c (10 sources loaded).

**Key decisions:**
- Human-gated default + autonomous opt-in (dual-mode, not full autonomy)
- Freddie-first, Foundry as proof-of-concept lens
- Agent SDK: evaluate now for leverage understanding, don't commit to migration
- Project code: AAR

input.content:
---
name: Worktree rebase before merge
description: Parallel worktrees branch from main at dispatch time — must rebase before merge if main moved
type: feedback
---

When dispatching multiple Ryans in parallel via worktrees, each worktree branches from main at the moment it's created. If earlier tasks merge to main between dispatch and completion, the later worktrees will have stale bases that revert merged changes in their diff.

**Why:** AAR Phase 1 Wave 2 — all 3 worktrees showed diffs reverting AAR-0001/0002 changes because they branched before those merges.

**How to apply:** After a worktree agent completes, always `git rebase main` in the worktree before reviewing the diff or merging. For worktrees that touch the same files (like brain.db schema), expect merge conflicts during rebase — resolve by keeping both sides (they're adding different things to the same file).

# Memory Index

## User & Feedback
- [User profile](user_profile.md) — Music producer/developer, iterative tester, Windows/nushell/Manila evenings
- [Communication style](feedback_communication_style.md) — Terse responses, no trailing summaries, show progress at milestones
- [Phase-gated execution](feedback_phase_gated_execution.md) — Stop at each phase boundary, user decides proceed/save/pause
- [No context fork](feedback_no_context_fork.md) — Skills run inline, forked contexts timeout on heavy workflows
- [API-first generation](feedback_api_first.md) — MiniMax agents must generate via API, not paste-ready files
- [Media in variations](feedback_media_in_variations.md) — Every variation file embeds its media with generations table
- [Track names curation](feedback_tracknames_curation.md) — Subagent keeps curating 18 names to 8 despite instructions
- [CLI-first everywhere](feedback_mcp_over_cli.md) — All tool calls use `bun run tool <name>`, MCP only for external/plugin
- [No compound commands](feedback_no_compound_commands.md) — Never cd, never &&, use `bun run tool <name>`, relative paths
- [Use relative paths](feedback_relative_paths.md) — Never prefix D:/PROJECTS/freddie-ai/, already in project root
- [No raw reference reads](feedback_no_raw_reference_reads.md) — Agents use brain.db/discover via tool runner, never Read on vault refs
- [Subagent discipline](feedback_subagent_discipline.md) — Drafts in out/, CLI only, lock bracket format per track
- [No skank in Suno](feedback_no_skank_suno.md) — "Skank" flagged by Suno, use "offbeat strum" or "offbeat chop"
- [Hook output behavior](feedback_hook_dual_output.md) — Hook stdout → context, stderr suppressed on exit 0
- [Agent voice attribution](feedback_skill_output_clean.md) — All agents speak with name prefix, tool output stays clean
- [Agent dispatch from skills](feedback_agent_dispatch.md) — Skills with agent: X must dispatch to subagent, never run inline
- [Recall strategy](feedback_recall_strategy.md) — FTS5 keyword before semantic/vector, use `bun run tool recall`
- [Project-local tasks](feedback_project_local_tasks.md) — Task docs in vault/studio/projects/{name}/tasks/, not vault/notes/tasks/
- [Worktree rebase](feedback_worktree_rebase.md) — Parallel worktrees need rebase before merge when main moves

## References
- [Statusline config](reference_statusline.md) — Custom statusline at ~/.claude/statusline.mjs, 3-row ANSI layout

## Known Issues
- [Bun test broken on Windows](project_bun_test_broken.md) — Segfault on import from modified modules, runtime works fine

## User Vision
- [Learning systems](user_vision_learning_systems.md) — Evolving AI that compounds knowledge, becomes deterministic
- [The Foundry](project_the_foundry.md) — Multi-agent Docker platform, 70-80% overlap with Shirozen

## Active Projects
- [OT-0015 ONNX Embeddings](project_onnx_embeddings.md) — Gemini→local ONNX, recall router, 384-dim. Pending --re-embed.
- [Shirozen SQLite decision](project_shirozen_sqlite_decision.md) — SQLite over Supabase, no Docker
- [Autonomous Agent Research](project_autonomous_agent_research.md) — AAR. Phase 1 complete 2026-04-07. Phase 2 (SDK POC) next.

## Completed Projects
- [Songwriting Lyrics Improvement](project_songwriting_lyrics_improvement.md) — 9-tool lyrics analyzer, 5 craft layers, trilingual. Complete 2026-04-05.
- [Agent Improvement](project_agent_improvement.md) — McCall/Holmes/Ryan deep personas, templates, protocols. Complete 2026-04-02.

input.content:
---
name: "2026-04-07 AAR Phase 1"
description: Self-learning autonomy — 7 tasks in one session, gates + judges + autonomous distill pipeline
type: project
---

Built the full autonomous self-learning pipeline in one session. 9 commits, ~2,500 lines of new code across 3 new libs, 1 new tool, and modifications to 8 existing files.

**Pipeline:** reflect (importance 7+ filter) → consolidate (autonomous) → distill → 5 gates → 3-model judge panel → auto-apply or stage for review → morning briefing shows results.

**Process:** Freddie architected → McCall sliced 7 task docs → Ryan implemented in worktrees (parallel where possible) → McCall reviewed → Freddie merged. Two code reviews caught real bugs (safe default for importance, isError propagation).

**New files:**
- `src/libs/gates.ts` — 5 validation gates (safety, size, drift, regression, constitution)
- `src/libs/judge-panel.ts` — Gemini + MiniMax + Grok, unanimous approval
- `src/libs/staged.ts` — staged proposal storage + brain.db sync
- `src/libs/autonomous-distill.ts` — end-to-end pipeline orchestrator
- `src/tools/autonomous-distill.ts` — CLI tool for the pipeline

**Modified:**
- `src/libs/reflect.ts` — importance scoring, shouldConsolidate autonomous mode
- `src/tools/consolidate-memory.ts` — --autonomous flag
- `src/tools/morning.ts` — Soul Updates section
- `src/libs/brain/` — soul_changes + staged_proposals tables, FTS5, sync
- `.claude/skills/bye/SKILL.md` — Phase 2.5 autonomous distill

**Parallel worktree lesson:** Worktrees branch from main at dispatch time. When merging sequential tasks to main between dispatches, later worktrees have stale bases. Always rebase before merge.
